what_it_does: A Supabase edge function that acts as a thin OAuth-facing forwarder for the Company MCP backend. It serves the OAuth protected-resource discovery doc (resource = this gateway, authorization server = the project's /auth/v1), a /healthz check, and an /mcp endpoint. On /mcp POST with no Bearer token it returns 401 with a WWW-Authenticate pointing at its own discovery; with a token it forwards the request verbatim (body + Authorization header) to the box backend (remote.py over Tailscale Funnel) and passes the response back, re-pointing WWW-Authenticate on a 401.
observations: Header comment (Tim 2026-06-22) declares a UNIFY decision: ONE brain in the box's remote.py; this function holds NO tool list and makes NO access decision
States the clients.allowed_tools column and the gateway's own dispatch() have been DELETED
BACKEND_URL defaults to https://workstation001.tail777bc2.ts.net, overridable via COMPANY_BACKEND_URL; described as a public Funnel URL, not a secret
Reads SUPABASE_URL from env; publicOrigin() returns it
Forwards user JWT verbatim; box re-validates (operator sub → all 66 tools; other valid user → posture-safe subset)
Returns 502 if the box is unreachable, with the error message included
Deno.serve binds to PORT (default 8000) and HOST (default 127.0.0.1)
standouts: Deliberately stateless/logic-free: explicitly a door, not a second brain — all auth and tool decisions deferred to the box
Exists only to keep a stable cloud OAuth resource URL while the real backend lives behind a Tailscale Funnel
conventions: Follows the OAuth protected-resource discovery + WWW-Authenticate bootstrap pattern
Forwarder/reverse-proxy pattern with verbatim header+body pass-through
Small helper json() for JSON responses; suffix-matching on url.pathname for routing
concerns: Uses pathname.endsWith() for route matching, which could match unintended longer paths
References '66 tools' and posture subset only in comments — actual numbers/logic not in this file
notes: A thin OAuth door: serves discovery + 401 bootstrap and forwards token-bearing /mcp requests verbatim to the box backend, which does all real auth and dispatch.
questions: What does the box's remote.py do with the forwarded JWT and how does it derive the posture-safe tool subset?
Why is HOST defaulted to 127.0.0.1 for a Supabase-deployed edge function?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the header says forwarder-only with no tool logic or auth decision, and the code only does discovery, 401 bootstrap, and verbatim forwarding.
apparent_themes: single source of truth / one brain
OAuth resource indirection (stable cloud URL fronting a home backend)
deny-by-default identity-gated access deferred to the backend
intention_signals: Seeks to eliminate duplicated tool menus and auth decisions by collapsing the gateway into a pure forwarder, keeping only the cloud-anchored OAuth resource the connecting app was bound to.
novelty: Pairs a Supabase edge function as the OAuth resource with a Tailscale-Funnel home backend as the actual MCP brain, forwarding JWTs across the boundary.
contribution: Contributes the stable cloud OAuth resource endpoint, discovery doc, 401/WWW-Authenticate bootstrap, and verbatim JWT-forwarding bridge to the box backend.
summary_for_embedding: A Supabase edge function forwarder that serves OAuth protected-resource discovery and forwards token-bearing /mcp requests verbatim to a Tailscale-Funnel box backend which performs all auth and tool dispatch.
intention_for_embedding: To keep a stable cloud OAuth resource URL for the MCP connector while unifying all tool logic and access decisions into the single backend brain on the box.
